comments.author: @ArroEL922
comments.text: This video focuses on the remote areas of Guyana (about 80%) . Guyana's population (approximately 836,000) is highly diverse, consisting primarily of five main ethnic groups: East Indian (39.8%), African (29.2%), Mixed (19.9%), Indigenous Amerindian (10.5%), and other groups including Portuguese, Chinese, and European (0.6%). Most of its population  live on a narrow strip of the country on the Northern (Atlantic) Coast.

replies.text: @WeathermanRoger I HAD TV IN 1983 FOR MY CHILDREN LIVED THE EARLY PART OF THEIR LIVES IN GUYANA WITH TV AND LAN LINE PHONE AND WATER AND ELECTRICITY

replies.author: @ArroEL922
replies.text: @WeathermanRoger Nice to hear.  Things have changed drastically, especially within the last few years.  I still have close relatives who live there.  Hope to visit soon.

replies.author: @WeathermanRoger
replies.text: ​@ArroEL922 Hi. I was there in 1983. Drove from Canada to Panama took a flight to Medelin and bus to Caracas, hitch hiked to BoVista Brazil, and flew from Guayana to Georgetown. Stayed 2 months. They never had TV yet and all spoke Englush. I can only soeak English. We had great beer conversations. CHEERS.

replies.author: @ArroEL922
replies.text: @WeathermanRogerYou are correct. I lived there for the first 20 years of my life in a somewhat "semi-civilized" area (a little  off the populated area but not quite in the interior as shown in this video) - no public electricity lines, no roads due to bad terrain, no potable water supply, no health centre, etc. but primary school available.  Haven't been back since 2001.  The YouTube channel https://www.youtube.com/@ITSOURLIFEARD has many better videos.

replies.author: @WeathermanRoger
replies.text: ​@ArroEL922 I spent time there. That thumbnail is Click Bait ! Cheap trick. That's a problem.
